events.title: Bharatanatyam - Indian Classical Dance Performance
events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

Language: Spanish

events.title: Music and Movement
events.description: Participants learn fundamental skills in note-reading, pitch, dance, singing, and Orff/Kodaly percussion techniques, as well as social skills like sharing, focus, teamwork, and listening. events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime

Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

Family Time runs from 11:00AM  to 11:30AM .  Accessibility Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events .

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 11:00am to 11:30am. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 11:30am to 12:00pm.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

The program is safe, supervised, and facilitated in partnership with North York Community House LEAP staff. events.title: Scientists in School: Science Meets Art
events.description: Discover what happens when science meets art! Explore perception as you use shadows, colour, motion, and light to create art from science. You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein) Please note: this program is suitable for children 4 years old and above with adult accompaniment. events.title: Rhythm and Poetry for teens
events.description: In this program led by spoken word coach Joshua Watkis, participants will learn the history and mechanics of Rhythm and Poetry (Rap), practice basic rhyme schemes and literary devices, and write and share their own 8-16 bar verse.
events.location: Oakwood Village Library and Arts Centre

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Come and play some fun PS5 or Nintendo Switch 2 games with your friends! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Pomodoro Study Hall
events.description: Join us for a focused study session using the Pomodoro Technique. Perfect for teens looking to boost productivity and stay on track with their schoolwork. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Drop in Resume Help
events.description: Need support making a resume for the summer? Bring it in for a touch up. Digital files preferred. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Gaming Café
events.description: Drop-in every Thursday to take part in video games and board games! Nintendo Switch and PS5 games available. All games available on a first come, first serve basis. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Flower Arrangements
events.description: Create beautiful flower bouquets! Welcome in the spring season with floral creating/designing. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, wher

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Anomia Tournament
events.description: Want to learn the fast-paced card game Anomia? Or test your skills against other youth? Come to the Parliament Street Youth Hub and compete in our Anomia tournament for a chance to win a cool prize. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Make Your Own Pillow
events.description: Join us for a hands-on workshop where you’ll learn the essential stitches to create a custom throw pillow. Not a fan of needles? No problem, use the cut and tie technique. Once your pillow is built, add personality and make it unique with fabric paint, buttons, or embroidery details. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Teen Quiet Study
events.description: Are you looking for a quiet space to study after school? Join our quiet study hall at Locke's program room every Thursday for individual or group study. Tables and chairs are provided, as well as a variety of materials to take study breaks. This is a drop-in program for Teens, and space is limited.

events.title: Youth Hub: Painting Sweet Keepsakes
events.description: Participants will be painting small ceramic moulds. This is a drop-in program. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop

events.title: Sea of Life: Documentary for National Canadian Film Day
events.description: Sea of Life is a 2017 documentary film about filmmaker Julia Barnes' journey around the world to save the ecosystems we depend on, as we come closer to causing a mass extinction in the oceans. Scheduled for the day after National Canadian Film Day, in this documentary, Barnes takes a deep dive into how actions by governments, businesses and ordinary people can all have a drastic impact on sustainability Thanks to Reel Canada  for providing the screening of this film. events.title: Japanese Tea Ceremony

The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. March 19: Michael Clayton (2007, 14A)  March 26: Ocean’s Twelve (2004, PG) April 2: Bridesmaids (2011, 14A) April 9: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery (1997, PG-13) April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration r

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. events.title: Toronto Games Week: An Intro to DaggerHeart
events.description: In celebration of Toronto Games Week (June 11 - Jun 17) come learn the ins and outs of the Tabletop Roleplaying phenomenon known as DaggerHeart. Players will fall into the roles of our heroic adventurers and prove they have the strength, skills, knowledge and bravery to become the Games Week Champions! No prior Tabletop Roleplaying experience required! Come with an open mind and an open heart! (Program intended for a mature audience.) Information Collection Notice: The personal information on th

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Come bring a book you are reading. We read for 30-45 minutes and then everyone talks about what they have read. We meet the second Thursday of every month from 7:00 to 8:00 pm.

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Parkdale branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. The tour will start and end at the Parkdale branch in the branch lobby area and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff. This program takes place rain or shine. events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café

events.title: D&D for Teens: On-going Campaign
events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens. Space limited for players*, but watchers are welcome too. All supplies provided. Bring a 5th level character or pick a Pre-generated character. *Register in-person at the Youth Hub at 3:30PM. events.title: Friday Teen Social
events.description: Drop in after school on Friday's to have fun with your friends, meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighborhood!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. events.title: Rulers in Space: Measuring Distances in the Universe
events.description: Have you ever wondered how we are able to measure distances in the Solar System, in our Galaxy, and beyond? Grab your cosmic ruler and embark on an exploration of the methods used to infer distances in the Universe — the result of centuries of innovative thinking by countless generations of astronomers. events.title: All Ages Karaoke and Board Game Night
events.description: Come sing your favourite tunes on our stage in a fun, encouraging environment. Request any song and we'll try to find a karaoke video and play it for you! If singing is not your thing, a few board games will be on hand if you want to try out Catan, Anomia, Ticket to Ride, or test your vocabulary with Scrabble! You are also welcome to bring your own and invite others to play with you. Children must be accompanied by an adult.

events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Candy Sculptures Art
events.description: Create with food and make your own artistic candy sculptures! Show your creativity then enjoy eating your your sculpture or bring them home.  This activity helps to build community and make creativity accessible and fun! Prepackaged candy will be provided.  Food allergy notice: Please be aware that food will be used in this program. Certain food ingredients may cause allergic reactions in some people.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Quiet Study Day
events.description: Every Tuesday the Kennedy/Eglinton Youth Hub becomes a quieter space for teens to study, work on projects and request help from staff. Ask for help connecting to BrainFuse for tutoring support and writing help.   This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to fin

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.
